observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to require:

The Municipal Treasurer to regularly provide the Municipal Accountant with tax delinquency data pertaining to RPT and SET;

The Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare an aging report of delinquent RPT and SET to determine impaired RPT/SET Receivables; and

Provide an allowance for impairment if warranted, to present an accurate account balance for these receivables in the FS of the Municipality by a debit to Deferred RPT/SET and a credit to Allowance for Impairment – RPT/SET Receivable.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain SLs of all real accounts and work back on the balances to reconcile and obtain information/details of the following:

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

Recommend to the Municipal Budget Officer the allocation of budget for the titling of lands owned by the Municipality; and

Direct the Municipal Engineer to facilitate the survey of lands, submit the survey plans to the Municipal General Service Officer, and proceed with the titling of such lands.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the BAC compute the NFCC of bidders using the audited FS of the preceding year and not based on the interim FS of the prospective bidders, moving forward, otherwise, said bidder shall be disqualified from obtaining an award or contract.




We recommended, and Management agreed to only issue the NOA upon obtaining the necessary authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to disburse funds for any project, to prevent any delays in its commencement.

We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:

To issue a memorandum order enjoining all officers and employees of the Municipality to strictly comply with Section 89 of PD No. 1445, with a demand to all those who have outstanding cash advances to liquidate the same immediately. Otherwise, authorize the Municipal Treasurer to withhold any claims against them until outstanding cash advances had been settled or liquidated.   

To stop the granting of cash advances to those officers and employees with outstanding cash advances; and

To file a request for write-off on the dormant cash advances amounting to P0.946 million in accordance with COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended, and Management agreed to notify COA of any deliveries of goods or services by submitting the AIR, PO, and other supporting documents within 24 hours after their acceptance, in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-010 and COA Circular No. 2009-002.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the liquidation report for the AMBaG Funds monthly in compliance with the MOA executed by and between the Municipality and OCM-BARMM.

We recommended, and Management agreed for the Municipal Treasurer to:

Require business owners to submit a sworn statement of gross sales/receipts during the business payment period and assessment of business taxes shall be made thereon;

Demand submission of ITR on or before April 30 as prescribed in the Local Ordinance and recompute assessment of business taxes. In case of deficiency of business taxes paid, demand collection thereof; and

Conduct examinations on the books of accounts of business taxpayers in cases of under declaration of gross receipts to establish effective enforcement and collection of taxes, fees, and charges as stipulated in the Ordinance.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the GAD focal point system (GFPS) thru the LCE endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan, the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313, which if passed, shall be posted in conspicuous places. Further, include in the ordinance the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions, b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof, and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to review the balances of Due to BIR and in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to remit immediately to the BIR the taxes withheld in full within the prescribed period, as provided under RMC No. 23-2012, to avoid penalties and charges. In the event that the BIR imposes penalties and surcharges for late filing and remittances, responsible officials for the delay will be held personally liable for the said amount.

We recommended for management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Establish RPT Receivable, Deferred RPT, SET Receivable and Deferred SET at gross amount every beginning of the year in accordance with Sections 19 and 20, Volume I NGAS;

We reiterated our recommendation for management to:

Instruct the Inventory Committee to:

prioritize the completion of physical count of PPEs; 

update the RPCPPE, reconcile the same with the Property Division/Unit and Accounting Unit records; and

Instruct the Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if any, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Make necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of affected accounts in the comparative financial statements in the ensuing year

We recommended for management to:

Require and demand from the Sparkotech Engineering Solutions Co. to pay to the BIR the amount of deficiency of withholding tax and submit proof/s of payment to COA; and

Comply with the withholding tax rate of fifteen percent (15%) for management and consultants in compliance with Section 2.57.2 (A) (6) of RR No. 11-2018.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that cash advances drawn for payment of job order workers are adequately supported with documents in accordance with Section 4 of PD 1445 and as enumerated in items 1.1.1 and 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended for management to:

Refrain from directly rewarding a contract to a supplier under negotiated procurement without invitation to at least three (3) suppliers and to follow the provisions related thereto in the 2016 RIRR and regulations of RA 9184; and

The BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documents as noted relating to the above transactions and to ensure its complete submission in all procurement activities."

We recommended for management to spend only for priority projects, programs and activities provided in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and R.A. No. 10121 so as to achieve the fund’s intended use and without prejudice to the budgetary constraints.

We recommended that Management be informed and updated of the relevant issuances by authorities that may affect their local governance performance scorecards like the DILG’s Seal of Good Local Governance and comply with the provisions therein like the submission of PSCP.

The Municipal Mayor to push through the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) in the preparation of engineering design and fully comply all the requirements for securing an Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) from the DENR-EMB to fully implement the disposal facility project.

The MENRO to communicate with the DENR-EMB, as follow up on the status of the recently submitted geo-tagged pictures on the proposed solid waste management site.

The municipality to comply with Republic Act No. 656 and other provisions of law in insuring its assets and properties with GSIS to secure against possible damage or loss. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor should implement the guidelines on the submission of Property Inventory Form as the basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage as enumerated in Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.

The Management to create a team to immediately and without delay, conduct a thorough investigation and analysis of prior years’ transactions to establish the causes of the discrepancy, which could be errors in recording and/or cash shortage of the accountable officers;

The OIC-Municipal Accountant’s Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office: 

To reconcile the accounts from prior years transactions to reflect the correct balances of accounts.

The Municipal General Service Officer (MGSO) to prepare the PAR/PTR for the donated medical equipment from the Barangay to the Municipality, and submit the same together with the RPCPPE to the accounting unit for recognition in the books; and
The Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the PAR/PTR and RPCPPE to record the donation recognizing the cost of the equipment and its relative depreciation.

The Municipal Engineer and the MGSO to facilitate the transfer of the Road Network from the Municipality to the Barangay, and provide the accounting unit the Certificate of Turn-over and PTR for derecognition in the books; and
The Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the transfer documents to record the transfer of the Road Network to the barangays, and draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the transfer of Road Networks in the barangay books of accounts.
